binomial_test_signal_bias.description: Binomial test: is female excess in sex-differential signals significant?
binomial_test_signal_bias.interpretation: Female-biased signals (51,771) significantly exceed male-biased (44,510), p < 1e-300 (binomial test, two-sided). Cohen's h = 0.0755 (small but highly significant due to N=96,281).

vs_faers_baseline.description: Test against FAERS reporting proportion (60.1% F)
vs_faers_baseline.interpretation: Even after adjusting for FAERS reporting bias (60.1% F), the observed 53.8% female proportion in signals is still significantly different (p = 0.00e+00).

drug_class_tests.description: Per-drug-class tests for sex bias distribution
drug_class_tests.method: Binomial test + chi-square goodness-of-fit vs 50/50 null

permutation_test_targets.description: Permutation test on 74 moderately biased targets (|sex_bias_score| >= 0.3, >= 3 drugs)

pathway_enrichment.description: Fisher exact test for pathway enrichment of F-biased vs M-biased drug targets
pathway_enrichment.fdr_method: Benjamini-Hochberg (separate for F and M enrichment)

ks_test_effect_magnitude.description: KS test: |log_ratio| distributions differ between F-biased and M-biased signals?
ks_test_effect_magnitude.interpretation: The magnitude of sex differences differs significantly between F-biased (mean |log_ratio|=1.0072) and M-biased (mean |log_ratio|=0.9631) signals (KS D=0.0387, p=1.42e-31).

all_comparisons_chi_square.description: Chi-square: F vs M direction across all 254,114 comparisons

key_findings.1_signal_female_bias: Female-biased signals (51,771) significantly exceed male-biased (44,510), p < 1e-300 (binomial, H0: 50/50). Also significant vs FAERS baseline (p = 0.00e+00).
key_findings.2_drug_classes: 18/18 drug classes show significant sex bias after FDR correction.
key_findings.3_target_permutation: 0/74 moderately biased targets survive FDR correction (permutation test, 10,000 iterations).
key_findings.4_pathway_enrichment: 32 pathways enriched for F-biased targets, 47 for M-biased targets (Fisher exact, FDR < 0.05).
